The Big Bad Wolf, or Bigby Wolf as he calls himself in The Wolf Among Us, is the main protagonist and playable character in the game. Long time ago in the Homelands, Bigby was born with a group of seven cubs to Winter Wolf after being courted by the powerful North Wind. He was often teased by his brothers, but loved greatly by his mother until the day she died. When the others went out looking for their father, Bigby stayed to protect the corpse from bounty hunters. At that time, he was too weak to defend anything.

Angered by the loss, he swore vengeance, promising to eat something bigger as each day passed until he became large enough to confront his father. Seven times he tried, and seven times he failed, eventually burying his hatred and grief deep inside his heart. Around that time he became known as "The Big Bad Wolf" of legend. He often terrorized Little Red Riding Hood, attempting to devour her as he was stopped by the Woodsman who cut him open and filled him up with stones. That's also when the two of them became enemies.

After a series of events, Snow White tracks Bigby down and asks him to come to Fabletown. There, he becomes the sheriff with a duty to protect the Fables from mundies (humans) as well as themselves. Many people are still scared of the Wolf because of his past, and has a hard time trusting him. 

As a bit of a loner, however, he tries to prove to everyone day after day that he's not the same person anymore, and that he's capable of being the sheriff that everyone needs.
generated_background_story: Corvin Graves was once a raven messenger spirit in the service of the ancient courts, ferrying secrets between the realm of mortals and the hidden world of mythical beings. His punishment for delivering a forbidden truth to a mortal lover was exile into permanent human form, stripped of his wings but retaining his preternatural perception and shadowy abilities. For decades, he has walked the rain-soaked streets of the city as a private investigator, using his knowledge of the hidden world to solve cases that blur the line between mundane crime and something far more sinister. When an ancient relic—the last remnant of the old laws that kept mythical and mortal worlds separate—is brutally destroyed along with its keeper, Corvin finds himself drawn into a conspiracy threatening to unravel the fragile peace. His internal struggle between ancient predatory instincts and his hard-won human conscience becomes a constant battle as he hunts for answers. Every decision he makes threatens to expose secrets that could destroy both worlds. The killer's trail leads through a web of mythical politics, old grudges, and betrayals that hit dangerously close to his own cursed past.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Shadow Sight: Corvin perceives hidden mythical auras and sees through supernatural disguises in the environment.
  - Raven's Whisper: Gathers rumors and secrets from the city's shadow network of informants and hidden sources.
  - Trench Coat Concealment: Stores improvised weapons, evidence, and tools without detection during searches.
- Tier 2:
  - Feathered Step: Silent movement allows Corvin to sneak past enemies and access restricted areas undetected.
  - Memory of Flight: Briefly levitates to reach high ledges, escape danger, or dodge incoming attacks.
  - Harbinger's Intuition: Predicts enemy movements in combat and identifies lies during interrogations.
- Tier 3:
  - Murder of Shadows: Summons a flock of shadow ravens to disorient, distract, and damage multiple enemies at once.
  - Ancient Oath: Once per episode, call upon old favors owed by powerful mythical beings for assistance or information.
  - True Form Awakening: Partially transforms into his raven spirit form, gaining enhanced speed, perception, and intimidation for a limited duration.

original_background_story:
Carley is one of the many main characters that appears in Telltale Games' The Walking Dead: Season One. She is a regional news reporter from Atlanta and a very skilled shooter. Carley's role is more active and she's never afraid to speak her mind. Having spent her time in war-zones as a correspondent, she presumably acquired basic knowledge of firearms and survival tactics that allows her to hold out during the outbreak.

Meeting Lee & Clementine in Macon, they later develop some kind of love relationship. Like the rest of the group, she cares about the members' welfare. Carley wants to save every survivor and give everyone a chance. Being very respectful and thoughtful, she reassures Lee that she will keep the secret to herself when he chooses to admit his past to her.

Not much is known about Carley's personal life, other than that she was raised Lutheran. The production crew drove to Macon to cover the Cherry Blossom Festival and that's when they were attacked by walkers. She was forced to watch her producer being eaten alive just a few feet away from her. Carley was saved by a guy called Doug.

Sadly, her life takes a turn for the worse as you get deeper into the story.
generated_background_story: Silas Wren was a high school history teacher in a quiet rural town before the collapse, known for his storytelling and stubborn optimism. When the crisis erupted, he lost his wife and daughter while attempting to evacuate, a burden of guilt he carries in every decision he makes. He spent the first year wandering aimlessly until he encountered a group of survivors who reminded him that protecting others was the only way to honor his family's memory. Silas now serves as a reluctant leader and mediator, using his knowledge of history to find parallels between past conflicts and their current struggles. His internal conflict centers on whether he truly believes in hope or simply performs it for others' sake, and the weight of every life lost under his watch. The episodic journey forces him to confront moral compromises he never imagined making, from choosing who receives limited supplies to deciding whether to trust strangers bearing gifts. Each episode peels back another layer of his composed exterior, revealing a man desperate for redemption but unsure if he deserves it.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Steady Hands: Reduces the tremor minigame difficulty when performing delicate tasks under pressure.
  - Teacher's Voice: Unlocks additional persuasive dialogue options when reasoning with hostile survivors.
  - Scavenger's Eye: Highlights interactive objects in the environment that would otherwise be missed.
- Tier 2:
  - Field Medicine: Allows Silas to stabilize injured characters, preventing certain story deaths.
  - History Repeats: Grants insight into faction motivations, revealing hidden agenda clues in conversations.
  - Quiet Steps: Reduces noise radius during stealth segments, making evasion sequences more forgiving.
- Tier 3:
  - Shepherd's Resolve: Once per episode, Silas can undo one major decision that resulted in a character's death.
  - Burden Bearer: Silas can carry additional inventory slots, allowing more flexible puzzle solutions.
  - Final Lesson: Unlocks a unique ending monologue reflecting on every major choice made throughout the story.

original_background_story:
Miranda Lawson is a human Cerberus Officer. As Jacob Taylor's informant, she gives him guidance and advice during missions, often via hologram. Miranda is very well traveled and has many contacts, extending from Citadel space and the Terminus Systems, to as far as the hostile Nemean Abyss. Her vast network of contacts may be explained by the fact Miranda is a known Cerberus operative, one who still has friends — or at least friends of friends — in the Alliance military. 
 
Miranda is a potential romantic interest for a male Commander Shepard.
generated_background_story: Jaxon Vane was once a celebrated tactical officer for the Interstellar Coalition until a catastrophic defeat cost him his battalion and his left eye, leaving him disgraced in the eyes of the public. Recalled from exile by a shadowy director, he is now the only hope to stop the "Harvesters," a synthetic hive-mind erasing organic life across the galaxy. Jaxon carries the weight of every soldier he has ever lost, driving him to make the ruthless decisions necessary for survival. He must navigate a web of political intrigue and recruit a team of assassins, biologists, and mercenaries who distrust both him and each other. His mission takes him to the galaxy's edge, a suicide run where he must sacrifice his own humanity to ensure the return of his crew. Failure means the total annihilation of all advanced civilizations.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Adrenaline Surge: Instantly regenerates health points when damage is taken.
  - Heavy Munitions: Increases inventory capacity for high-caliber ammunition.
  - Command Presence: Unlocks basic dialogue options to intimidate enemies into fleeing.
- Tier 2:
  - Interrupt Protocol: Allows the user to instantly attack or trigger traps during enemy dialogue.
  - Tactical Cloak: Grants temporary invisibility to flank enemies or avoid detection.
  - Unified Front: Squadmates receive a defense bonus based on the Commander's reputation.
- Tier 3:
  - Last Rites: Massive damage boost when health is critical, allowing for a final stand.
  - Galactic Diplomat: Guarantees success in high-stakes negotiation encounters.
  - Omega Bombardment: Calls in an orbital strike that devastates a large area of the battlefield.

original_background_story: Mario Auditore (1434 – 1500) was the leader of the Italian Brotherhood of Assassins from at least 1476 to his death, a condottiero, and the ruler of Monteriggioni from 1454 until his death.
generated_background_story: Born into a prestigious lineage of merchant lords in the canal city of Vespera, Alessio Vane enjoyed a life of luxury until a ruthless political coup left his family decimated and his name ruined. Orphaned and hunted by the ruling Council, he found refuge in the forgotten underground tunnels of the city, where a secretive guild of freedom fighters trained him in the arts of shadows and silent death. Driven by a burning desire for retribution, Alessio now uses his high-status knowledge and lethal skills to dismantle the conspiracy that betrayed him. His fight takes him across the rooftops and through the canals, turning the city's architecture into a weapon of justice. Though he fights for the people, his soul is tormented by the violence he must inflict to achieve peace. The lines between heroic liberation and personal vengeance blur as he climbs the ranks of the conspiracy, targeting corrupt nobles one by one. Ultimately, he must decide if he is fighting to save the city or just to satisfy his own grief.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Swift Movement: Increases sprint speed and enables basic climbing grips for faster traversal.
  - Hidden Dagger: Allows for silent takedowns when approaching enemies from behind undetected.
  - Coin Toss: Throws a coin to distract nearby guards, creating openings for escape or assassination.
- Tier 2:
  - Smoke Veil: Deploys a smoke bomb to blind enemies, allowing for quick escapes or counter-attacks.
  - Counter Strike: Parry an enemy's attack during combat to instantly break their defense.
  - Rooftop Acrobat: Reduces fall damage and allows for extended ledge hanging for better positioning.
- Tier 3:
  - Phantom Bolt: Fires a silent projectile from a hidden wrist mechanism to eliminate distant targets.
  - Shadow Blend: Becomes nearly invisible while standing still in dark corners or crowds.
  - Chain Assassination: Allows chaining instant kills between multiple adjacent enemies in rapid succession.

original_background_story:
Noel Kreiss is a playable character of Final Fantasy XIII-2, and a supporting character in Lightning Returns: Final Fantasy XIII.

When Noel was born in the Farseer tribe (that lived a nomadic life), he was the second to last child. After the Cocoon came crashing down, it's crystal shattering, the event left the world in ruins and warfare broke out that killed the majority of mankind.

When Noel travels back in time, he's the last remaining human being from 700 AF. He goes back to the past in order to try and change the outcome of his existence. He meets Lightning in Valhalla, who tells Noel about her sister and how she wants him to help them reunite while changing the future. Doing as he was told, he then goes to meet with Serah and quickly becomes her friend.

At first, Noel is cocky and confident, yet cheerful and soft-spoken. He's a self-sufficient fighter and a hunter that sadly suffers from amnesia thanks to the time paradox. Coming from a future where humanity is almost extinct, he has some trouble with his speech, so he tends to speak in a straightforward manner. The boy loves meeting new people and explore new places. Being very protective of others, he quickly starts caring for Serah and tries his best to protect her. Noel stands by his beliefs and morals, and as a remnant from his upbringing, values life and is reluctant to hurt or kill others unless necessary. This is the reason why he doesn't like Serah's boyfriend Snow, who has a very brash personality with the heads in the clouds.
generated_background_story: Elara Vane awoke in the desolate streets of the Hollow District with no memory of her past, her wrist branded with a glowing countdown of thirteen days. She is a Harvester, a rare individual capable of extracting spiritual essence from the dying world to power the Ark, a massive vessel destined for a new world. Armed with her customized Aether Gauntlets, she fights through hordes of corrupted specters that feed on the remaining life force of the city. Every soul she saves grants the Ark the energy needed to ascend, but the process physically drains her own vitality. With the clock ticking towards zero, she must make impossible choices about who is worthy of boarding the Ark before the final darkness consumes everything. She is constantly haunted by visions of a life she cannot remember, driving her forward to understand why she was chosen for this burden. As the days pass, Elara realizes that saving the world might require the ultimate sacrifice of her own existence.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Soul Siphon: Elara drains health from enemies to replenish her own reserves during combat.
  - Flash Step: A quick dash ability that allows her to phase through solid objects and enemy attacks.
  - Spirit Sense: Highlights hidden collectibles and enemy weak points within a short radius.
- Tier 2:
  - Kinetic Burst: Releases a shockwave of energy from the gauntlets that knocks back surrounding enemies.
  - Chrono Lock: Temporarily freezes a single target in time, leaving them vulnerable to attack.
  - Spectral Shield: Creates a barrier of souls that absorbs a set amount of damage before breaking.
- Tier 3:
  - World Ender: Unleashes the full power of the gauntlets in a massive area-of-effect explosion of light.
  - Ascension Protocol: Doubles the duration of the day cycle, granting more time to explore before the countdown advances.
  - Final Rite: Revives Elara instantly with full health upon fatal damage, but resets the soul counter to zero.

original_background_story:
Kratos was born in Sparta and became a fierce warrior. In a desperate moment during battle, he pledged his life to Ares in exchange for victory, becoming his servant. Ares, manipulating Kratos, caused him to slaughter his own wife and daughter, leading to Kratos’ deep hatred for the gods.

Fueled by rage and guilt, Kratos renounced Ares and embarked on a quest to destroy him, ultimately becoming the new God of War. His journey didn't end there; seeking revenge against the entire pantheon, Kratos waged war against the gods of Olympus, resulting in the destruction of the Greek world.

Years later, Kratos finds himself in the Norse realm, seeking a peaceful life with his son, Atreus. Despite his attempts to leave his past behind, he becomes entangled in the conflicts of the Norse gods. This new journey focuses on his relationship with Atreus and his struggle with his own violent nature as he faces gods and monsters of Norse mythology.
generated_background_story: Valerius was once the supreme commander of the Obsidian Legion, a military order sworn to the "Eternal Architects." During a campaign to purge a corrupted region, he was manipulated by his masters via a magical geas to slaughter the inhabitants, only to discover they were his own family disguised by an illusion. Horrified by the deed and the betrayal, Valerius shattered the binding oath of loyalty with sheer will, causing the magical brand on his chest to scar over. He was subsequently imprisoned in the Abyssal Keep, a jail for those who defy the Architects. Now, Valerius must fight his way out, severing the lingering psychic bonds that allow the Architects to track him. His motivation is not just freedom, but the complete annihilation of the organization that stole his life.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Oathbreaker's Rage: Valerius enters a frenzy where attacks automatically chain together until the player ceases input or takes damage.
  - Hydro-Lung: A magical discipline that allows Valerius to breathe underwater and move through deep currents without obstruction.
  - Chain Bind: Valerius uses his broken shackles to pull distant enemies closer or latch onto high ledges for traversal.
- Tier 2:
  - Chronos Amulet: Valerius manipulates time to decay ancient barriers or slow down fast-moving mechanical traps within the environment.
  - Shadow Resonance: Creates a shadow duplicate that mimics Valerius's attacks, effectively doubling his damage output for a short duration.
  - Illusion Dispel: Reveals hidden passageways and dispels magical constructs created by the Architects to confuse the player.
- Tier 3:
  - Elemental Infusion: Imbues Valerius's axe with fire, ice, or lightning, allowing him to exploit enemy weaknesses and unlock specific doors.
  - Divine Allegiance: Valerius aligns with a forgotten deity, gaining a unique ultimate ability based on the chosen god's domain.
  - Arsenal Mastery: Allows Valerius to equip and swap between two distinct main weapons during combat without opening the menu.

original_background_story:
Emil, also known as No. 7, is a companion and deuteragonist in NieR, its remake, NieR Replicant ver.1.22474487139..., and a minor character in NieR:Automata.

A young boy that joins Nier's party towards the end of Act I, cursed with the ability to turn anyone who sees his eyes to stone. He is the younger twin brother of Halua.

In Act II, Emil reveals himself to be No.7, an ancient weapon created through human experimentation. He is ultimately transformed into a skeleton-like being after fusing with Halua, but retains his soul and gains new powers.

In NieR:Automata, Emil is revealed to have survived the events of NieR. A disembodied copy of Emil is briefly encountered inside the head of a machine, and is later seen attached to a moving sales cart where he offers his wares for the roaming androids of YoRHa. He recovers his memories with the androids' help and reveals he and countless other Emils were created by the original Emil to fight against the aliens.
generated_background_story: Unit K-07 was manufactured by the orbital Archive as a vanguard model designed specifically for surface reconnaissance and heavy machine elimination. Dropped into the decaying ruins of a once-thriving metropolis, her primary directive is to locate and destroy the Central Core that perpetuates the machine dominance. Unlike earlier models, K-07 possesses a heuristic emotional processor, causing her to question the necessity of the war she was built to fight. She fights alongside a hovering tactical drone that provides tactical analysis and ranged fire support. The machines she encounters are erratic, often mimicking human behaviors that confuse her logical subroutines. As she journeys through the dystopia, she discovers that the machines are evolving into something more than just cold metal. Her internal conflict deepens when she realizes that victory might require the sacrifice of her own evolving consciousness. The stakes rise as she must choose between fulfilling her coded destiny or preserving the strange new life growing within the machine network.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Thermal Edge: Adds a burning effect to melee attacks, causing damage over time to machine enemies.
  - Pulse Shot: The support drone fires a concussive energy blast that interrupts enemy attacks.
  - Dash Module: A quick lateral movement ability that allows K-07 to phase through incoming projectiles.
- Tier 2:
  - EMP Burst: Releases a localized electromagnetic pulse that disables enemy shields and slows movement.
  - Repair Protocol: Activates a nanobot cloud that regenerates structural integrity over a short duration.
  - Counter-Reflect: A defensive stance that absorbs incoming damage and reflects it back as a shockwave.
- Tier 3:
  - Overclocked Reflexes: Temporarily slows down time perception, allowing for rapid consecutive strikes.
  - Gravity Well: Deploys a singularity that pulls multiple enemies into a single cluster for area damage.
  - Final Resonance: A high-damage area attack that consumes all energy reserves to unleash a devastating blade beam.

original_background_story:
The G-Man is a mysterious and enigmatic character encountered several times in the Half-Life series. Referred to as a "sinister interdimensional bureaucrat",[3] he is known to display peculiar behavior and to wield powers far beyond those of a human. His identity and motives remain completely unexplained. His allegiance also remains unknown to this day. 
He plays the role of an overseer and eventual employer, watching over Gordon Freeman and other characters as the series progresses, at times helping or hindering them.
generated_background_story: Kira Vance was a promising theoretical physicist at the Apex Research Complex, specializing in dimensional field manipulation. When an unauthorized experiment triggered a cascade breach, reality itself began to unravel, warping the facility and transforming colleagues into hostile aberrations. Kira survived the initial collapse through quick thinking and an experimental displacement gauntlet that now fuses with her nervous system. The gauntlet allows her to perceive and briefly manipulate local spacetime, making her uniquely equipped to navigate the corrupted halls. She seeks to reach the Core Chamber and reverse the breach before it expands beyond containment. Internally, she battles guilt over her indirect role in enabling the experiment and the faces of friends she must now put down. The facility's deteriorating stability means every path she takes shifts and reconfigures. Her presence destabilizes dimensional weak points further, making stealth impossible and forcing constant adaptation. She is not a soldier, but survival has sharpened her into something formidable.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Pulse Sidearm: Fires concentrated energy rounds that stagger enemies and can disable electronic locks.
  - Field Sense: Highlights interactive objects and enemy positions through walls for a brief duration.
  - Improvised Armor: Dismantles facility debris to craft temporary damage-absorbing plating.
- Tier 2:
  - Displacement Dash: Short-range teleport that leaves behind a disorienting distortion field.
  - Kinetic Rebound: Absorbs incoming projectile energy and reflects it back as a concussive blast.
  - Systems Hack: Overloads machinery and automated turrets to fight on her behalf temporarily.
- Tier 3:
  - Temporal Stasis: Freezes all entities and hazards within a localized sphere for several seconds.
  - Cascade Nova: Unleashes stored dimensional energy in an expanding ring that disintegrates hostile matter.
  - Reality Anchor: Creates a stabilized zone where health regenerates and dimensional anomalies cannot enter.

original_background_story:
Dante is the primary protagonist of the Devil May Cry series created and published by Capcom. In the first four games, Dante is a private investigator and a demon hunting vigilante. He want to track down and kill those who murdered his mother and corrupted his brother. He is the son of Sparda who is a demon of great power. That's why Dante possesses numerous powers beyond that of any human. He can use a combination with a variety of weapons to accomplish his goals.

Named after the Italian poet Dante Alighieri, the character was designed to fit Devil May Cry game designer Hideki Kamiya's vision of a "cool and stylish" man, and his personality was based on the title character of the manga series Cobra. Dante's arsenal usually consists of firearms and melee weapons, including "Ebony and Ivory", twin handguns that never need reloading, as well as a variety of swords such as the "Rebellion" and "Force Edge", Devil Arms created by his father.

Dante has also become one of the most popular characters in gaming, having been recognized for his role in the Devil May Cry series and various characteristics that have attracted gamers. However, Dante's redesign and characterization in the reboot was the subject of controversy amongst gamers.
generated_background_story: Born in the smog-choked metropolis of Ironhive, Kaelen grew up on the streets, unaware of his lineage as a Riftborn—a hybrid of human and the ethereal entities known as Silhouettes. He lived a life of petty crime until a government purge revealed his true nature, forcing him to go on the run. Unlike the heroes of ancient legends, Kaelen is cynical and abrasive, fighting not for honor but to tear down the corrupt corporations that control the city. He wields the Fulcrum, a shape-shifting mechanical blade that channels his innate energy, allowing him to slaughter waves of robotic enforcers and corrupted spirits. His internal struggle centers on the creeping corruption of his own humanity; every time he taps into his Riftborn powers, he risks losing his mind to the Silhouette consciousness. The stakes are personal: if he falls, the entity within him will open a gate that will swallow his world whole. Driven by a desire for vengeance and the faint hope of a normal life, he cuts a bloody path through the city's elite.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Rift Dash: Kaelen instantly teleports a short distance forward to evade attacks or close gaps.
  - Iron Eruption: He slams the Fulcrum into the ground, creating a shockwave that knocks nearby enemies off their feet.
  - Spirit Drain: Landing consecutive hits on enemies restores a small amount of health.

  - Aerial Cleave: Kaelen performs a powerful spinning attack in mid-air, allowing him to stay airborne while damaging foes.
  - Chain Reaction: Successfully hitting an enemy causes a chance for lightning to arc to a nearby secondary target.

original_background_story:
Christa is a main character who first appears in Telltale Games's The Walking Dead: Season One, played by voice actress Mara Junot. She is much more serious than her boyfriend Omid, but loves him deeply and is concerned for the safety of the group. After Lee's death, Christa and Omid take up Lee's role as Clementine's guardians.

Little is known about Christa's life before the outbreak. She lived in a San Francisco apartment, and she and her boyfriend, Omid, were taking the "All-American Road Trip" when the apocalypse began...
generated_background_story: Elara Vance was a third-year medical student when the outbreak transformed civilization into a landscape of ruin and wandering threats. Separated from her younger sister during the chaotic evacuation of their home city, she has spent the last two years searching every settlement and safe zone for any trace of her family. Her medical training has made her a valuable asset to the scattered groups of survivors she encounters along the way. She carries a battered field journal filled with names and descriptions of people she has promised to help or find. The weight of impossible choices, including those that cost lives during close calls with the infected, haunts her dreams and tests her resolve. She struggles to balance her instinct to heal with the brutal pragmatism required to stay alive. Every conversation is a potential lifeline or a trap, and she has learned to read people the way she once read medical charts. Her journey is driven by love, but her internal conflict centers on whether she can remain human in a world that demands monstrous decisions.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Triage: Elara can stabilize injured allies, preventing their condition from worsening during critical moments.
  - Keen Eye: Highlights interactive objects and hidden clues in the environment for a short duration.
  - Persuasion: Unlocks additional dialogue options when negotiating with hostile or distrustful survivors.
- Tier 2:
  - Improvised Medicine: Allows Elara to craft healing items from common scavenged materials.
  - Distraction: Elara can throw objects to create noise and divert attention away from her position.
  - Quick Reflexes: Grants a longer time window to complete quick time events during action sequences.
- Tier 3:
  - Leadership: Nearby allied non-player characters receive a bonus to their effectiveness in combat and dialogue.
  - Silent Takedown: Elara can eliminate lone threats without alerting others in the vicinity.
  - Second Chance: Once per chapter, Elara survives a failed quick time event that would otherwise result in a restart.

original_background_story: Knuckles the Echidna from the Sonic the Hedgehog franchise by Sega.
generated_background_story: Vira grew up in the cliffside settlement of Oakhaven, where storytellers spoke of the ancient World-Serpent that once swallowed the sun and doomed the world to shadow. A failed guardian ritual in her youth bound her soul to the Skyweave, a living energy cord passed down by her forgotten bloodline of temple wardens. Now she carries the weight of a fractured lineage, haunted by memories of her older sibling who vanished while attempting the same ritual years ago. When seismic tremors signal that the World-Serpent is stirring from its subterranean slumber, Vira must descend into the Overgrown Depths to reassemble the scattered keystones before the beast can rise. Her internal struggle pits personal vengeance against the greater good, as the same power she needs to defeat the Serpent may also be the key to saving—or erasing—what remains of her family.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Skyweave Swing: Latch onto glowing anchors to swing across wide chasms and reach higher platforms.
  - Tether Pull: Yank enemies out of formation or pull distant items into reach with a quick energy lash.
  - Slipstream Dash: Slide a short distance to evade attacks or slip through tight environmental gaps.
- Tier 2:
  - Voltagrip: Electrify the Skyweave to stun enemies caught in its grasp for a brief duration.
  - Anchor Launch: Create a temporary anchor point mid-air to swing again and extend aerial traversal.
  - Coil Shield: Wrap the Skyweave around Vira to absorb one incoming hit before breaking.
- Tier 3:
  - Stormweaver: Charge the Skyweave with accumulated ambient energy for increased damage and area effect.
  - Skyward Bind: Suspend multiple enemies in mid-air leaving them vulnerable to follow-up attacks.
  - World-Tether Cataclysm: Unleash a sweeping energy wave that damages all nearby enemies and reshapes certain terrain obstacles.

original_background_story:
Circus Baby (commonly referred to as Baby) is the secondary antagonist of the Five Nights at Freddy’s series and the animatronic mascot of Circus Baby's Pizza World invented by Afton Robotics, LLC and made for Fazbear Entertainment, Inc.

Baby is haunted by the soul of Elizabeth Afton, who was the daughter of the serial killer William Afton. However, during the completion ending, Elizabeth's soul was presumably set free.
generated_background_story: Elliot Vance took the night shift at the newly reopened "Fantasy Fun Center" out of financial desperation, ignoring the rumors surrounding the previous owner's disappearance. During his first week, he discovered the attraction's vintage animatronics housed a dark, sentient energy that awakened after midnight. Tasked with surviving until 6 AM, Elliot must navigate the building's labyrinthine security office and corridors using only a flashlight and the facility's unreliable power grid. He quickly realizes these machines are not just malfunctioning, but are actively hunting him with predatory intelligence. As he uncovers maintenance logs hidden in the backrooms, his motivation shifts from earning a paycheck to uncovering the truth behind the "accidents" that plague the location. Elliot fights to maintain his sanity against hallucinations caused by sleep deprivation and the mascots' supernatural influence. If he fails, he risks becoming a permanent fixture in the attraction's twisted history.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Flashlight Focus: Conserves battery life and increases the range of the light beam.
  - Quick Lock: Instantly secures the security doors to block incoming threats for a short duration.
  - Sprint: Grants a temporary burst of speed to escape close encounters with animatronics.
- Tier 2:
  - Audio Distraction: Hacks the intercom to play sounds in other rooms to lure enemies away.
  - Signal Jammer: Temporarily disables electronic locks on animatronics to keep them frozen in place.
  - Thick Skin: Reduces the panic effect when looking directly at the animatronics.
- Tier 3:
  - EMP Burst: Releases a powerful electromagnetic pulse that stuns all electronics in the vicinity.
  - Reboot Protocol: Automatically restores power to critical systems after a blackout.
  - Escape Artist: Provides a rare second chance to survive a jump scare encounter.

original_background_story:
Geralt of Rivia (or Geralt z Rivii in Polish) is a witcher and the main protagonist of The Witcher game series by CD Project RED. However, he originally comes from a series of books with the same name, written by Andrzej Sapkowski. As a witcher and a monster hunter, you can hire him for anything, as long as he gets paid for it. Geralt possesses nearly superhuman abilities and is an expert with swords. During The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt (in 1272), Geralt's mentor Vesemir says that he is "nearly a century old".

Geralt's mother, Visenna, left him with the witchers in Kaer Morhen, where he grew up. During this time, to appear more trustworthy to future clients, young witchers were encouraged to make up surnames for themselves. Geralt wanted to pick "Geralt Roger Eric du Haute-Bellegarde", but Vesemir saw the names as silly and unprofessional, leaving him with Geralt, and Geralt only. Even though he didn't come from Rivia, "Of Rivia" was a practical alternative as a last name. Geralt adopted the name and even started to talk with a Rivian accent so that he would appear more authentic. Except from that name, the dryads gave him the nickname "The White Wolf".

During the "Trial of the Grasses", Geralt survived numerous mutations, and he was one of the few who survived. That's why his hair is white (no pigment), and his senses better and stronger than others. When his witcher training was over, he embarked on his horse called Roach (giving the same name to every one of his horses), to become the slayer of monsters.

For his services, as a reward, Geralt wanted the unborn child of princess Pavetta. However, when he found out that it was a girl, he decided not to take her. But fate caused Geralt and Pavetta's daughter Ciri to cross paths multiple times, and after the death of queen Calanthe (Ciri's grandmother), The White Wolf decided to take care of the girl. He soon came to love her as his own daughter.

Geralt's love of his life is called Yennefer of Vengerberg, and he also has a really close friend that goes by the name Dandelion. He's a poet and a bard with a wild imagination and a love for girls.
generated_background_story: Valerius Thorne was born into a caste of magically altered wardens, trained from childhood to hunt the beasts that stalk the borderlands between warring nations. While serving as a personal bodyguard for the Northern Monarch, he was knocked unconscious during a chaotic coup, awakening to find the King dead and the murder weapon in his hand. Framed for the assassination by a shadowy cabal seeking to destabilize the region, he is now hunted by both the crumbling Northern Alliance and the invading Obsidian Empire. Valerius must navigate a treacherous political landscape, using his unique sensory abilities to track the true killer. His journey is complicated by the slow erosion of his humanity caused by the very mutations that make him a formidable fighter. With the continent on the brink of total war, he seeks to uncover the truth not for glory, but to silence the whispers that brand him a kingslayer. Every step closer to the truth forces him to confront the moral ambiguity of the leaders he once blindly served.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Runic Cleave: Valerius infuses his blade with energy to deliver a sweeping attack that hits multiple enemies in front of him.
  - Hunter's Sense: Activates a heightened sensory mode allowing the player to see enemy weak points and hidden tracks in the environment.
  - Defensive Stance: Valerius braces for impact, reducing incoming physical damage and restoring a small amount of stamina for each hit blocked.
- Tier 2:
  - Shadow Step: A quick teleportation ability that allows Valerius to dash behind an enemy instantly, granting a temporary damage bonus for backstabs.
  - Arcane Trap: Deploys a magical snare on the ground that immobilizes the first enemy to walk over it and applies a damage-over-time effect.
  - Alchemical Adaptation: Passive skill that grants resistance to status ailments like poison and bleed, converting a portion of damage taken into stamina.
- Tier 3:
  - Avatar of Vengeance: Temporarily transforms Valerius into a spectral wraith, drastically increasing attack speed and allowing his attacks to ignore enemy armor.
  - Chrono-Stasis: Creates a localized field that slows down time for all enemies within a radius, allowing for strategic repositioning or uninterrupted combos.
  - Executioner's Resolve: A passive mastery skill where every kill restores a significant portion of health and cooldowns, turning Valerius into a relentless force on the battlefield.

original_background_story: Delilah is the deuteragonist of Firewatch and the supervisor of Henry, the game's protagonist. Henry remains in nearly constant contact with her via radio and can be reached at any time she or Henry starts a conversation or Henry finds something worth of reporting to her, except during sections taking place in the cave. As the game progresses, increasing doubt is shed on Delilah, her relationship with Henry, her affiliation and what she potentially knows. As such, the player has the choice when making discoveries to report to her or not. The player never meets Delilah in person, leaving her appearance a mystery.
generated_background_story: Elias Thorne grew up in a quiet coastal town where days were measured by the rhythm of fishing boats and fog rolling in from the sea. After a series of personal losses and a relationship that ended in bitter silence, Elias made the impulsive decision to spend a summer as a fire lookout in the remote mountain wilderness, seeking solitude and a chance to think. The job seemed simple enough: watch for smoke, report anything unusual, and maintain the tower. But as the weeks stretched on, Elias began noticing odd patterns in the forest—unexplained lights at night, strange radio interference, and footprints that appeared where no one should have walked. The only lifeline to the outside world is a voice on the other end of a handheld radio, a supervisor stationed at another tower, whose casual observations gradually turn into concerned questions about what Elias might be seeing. Elias must navigate not only the physical dangers of the wilderness but also the creeping sense that something larger is at play, something that may be connected to the very reasons for fleeing to the mountains in the first place. The summer becomes a journey of confronting old wounds while questioning what is real and what is merely the echo of a troubled mind seeking patterns in chaos.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Orienteering: Reveals hidden trails and paths on the map when surveying from high vantage points.
  - Keen Sight: Highlights interactive objects and points of interest within a wider visual radius.
  - Radio Protocol: Reduces static interference during transmissions, unlocking additional dialogue options with the supervisor.
- Tier 2:
  - Tracker's Instinct: Allows identification of footprints and disturbances, revealing how recently an area was visited.
  - Cartographer: Automatically logs detailed terrain features and landmarks when exploring new locations.
  - Steady Hand: Enables longer focus through binoculars without visual drift or stamina loss.
- Tier 3:
  - Sixth Sense: Creates a subtle visual cue when something significant is nearby but not yet visible.
  - Signal Decrypter: Picks up faint mysterious radio transmissions that reveal hidden story fragments.
  - Wilderness Endurance: Reduces stamina drain when traversing difficult terrain or during adverse weather conditions.

original_background_story:
Good looking, smooth as silk, and silver-tongued, Mike is the resident ladykiller. He's had more girlfriends than he's had hot dinners, which can cause a few problems when a few of them are in his immediate circle of friends...

Mike is a man of action. He's always the first to volunteer, the first to make a suggestion. With a narcissistic streak, there's no denying that he likes the attention that comes with being the man who stands up and can get things done.
generated_background_story: Kaelen was once a stalwart guardian of the Sacred Spire, sworn to protect the six sisters whose songs maintained the world's equilibrium. When the eldest sister unleashed her treacherous rampage, Kaelen was the sole survivor of the initial onslaught, left for dead amidst the ruins of his order. He was discovered by Rhyx, a rogue dragon cast out by the same magical discord that now threatens the realm. Forming a pact of mutual survival and vengeance, Kaelen and Rhyx now roam the war-torn lands, hunting the fiends summoned by the corrupted song. Kaelen wields a heavy greatsword, relying on brute strength and aerial support from Rhyx to combat the magical horrors. He struggles with the guilt of failing his duty and the realization that the "peace" he protected was built on lies. His ultimate goal is to confront the eldest sister, not to restore the old order, but to forge a new world free from the manipulation of divine song. The bond between rider and dragon is his only anchor in a reality gone mad.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Iron Cleave: A powerful overhead slash that crushes enemy armor and deals heavy damage.
  - Shadow Dash: A quick evasion move that allows Kaelen to pass through enemies unharmed.
  - Dragon's Breath: Rhyx provides a short-range burst of fire to knock back surrounding foes.
- Tier 2:
  - Cyclone Strike: Kaelen spins his greatsword rapidly, hitting all enemies in a wide radius.
  - Sky Dive: Kaelen leaps into the air and slams down, creating a shockwave upon impact.
  - War Cry: A roar that boosts attack power for a short duration while stunning nearby enemies.
- Tier 3:
  - Crimson Fury: A relentless chain of attacks that increases in speed with every hit landed.
  - Rhyx's Wrath: Rhyx descends to carpet bomb the battlefield with powerful fireballs.
  - Songbreaker: A finishing move that nullifies magic in the area and deals critical damage to bosses.

original_background_story:
This character is from TES 5 Skyrim mod called Bruma. 
 
A khadjit who lives in Bruma. Originally from Elsweyr Dar'taqto has lived most of his life in Cyrodil. He says that he moved from Elsweyr so long ago that does not rememder what the reason was. Maybe he just doens't want to tell the reason. 
 
Dar'taqto seems to have warm feelings towards Elsweyr. He can even tell the protagonist "You are to Dar'taqto as the sun is to Elsweyr... I mean - a good friend". 
He says that he would like to become emperor one day. It seems that he is dissapointed in current emperor. 
 
Dar'taqto gets banned from Bruma but the protagonist can help him by taking him along as follower. 
 
He fights enemies with two battle axes. Dar'taqto wears light armor. 
 
WARNING: The outfit on the picture is not his original outfit. The picture is taken at Skaal Village, Soltheim.
generated_background_story: Born in a border town that was constantly disputed by neighboring warlords, Kaelen Varr grew up resenting the artificial lines drawn on maps that divided families and cultures. He spent his youth learning the old paths and forgotten trails that circumvented the official, guarded borders. When the magical seals isolating the provinces began to weaken, Kaelen felt a resonant hum in the ancient stones of his homeland, calling him to explore the "Roots of the World." He now serves as a guide for those seeking passage into the "Seat of Sundered Kings," hoping to unite the fractured lands through shared knowledge rather than bloodshed. Driven by the disappearance of his mentor in the "Star-Wounded East," he risks life and limb to chart the dangerous routes to the "Lure of the Wilds." Kaelen struggles with the guilt of leading others into dangerous lands, questioning if he is a pathfinder or merely a magnet for trouble. His ultimate goal is to complete the Atlas of the Open Road, proving that no border is truly impassable.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Border Stride: Allows the character to traverse difficult terrain like snow, sand, and swamp without movement penalties.
  - Surveyor's Eye: Highlights hidden paths and secret entrances on the mini-map for a short duration.
  - Wayfinder's Mark: Places a magical beacon allowing instant return to a specific safe location.
- Tier 2:
  - Provincial Insight: Grants temporary resistance to elemental damage based on the specific region currently being explored.
  - Diplomatic Immunity: Reduces hostility of local wildlife and factions for a limited time after entering a new province.
  - Path of the Wanderer: Increases stamina regeneration significantly while traveling on foot outside of combat.
- Tier 3:
  - Ley Line Transit: Teleports the character instantly between discovered landmarks within the same province.
  - Cartographer's Gambit: Reveals the full layout of an unexplored dungeon or cave and highlights all treasure chests.
  - Union of Lands: Summons a spirit ally from the nearest neighboring province to assist in combat for a short duration.

original_background_story:
Elizabeth is a character and A.I. companion in BioShock Infinite.
With the game set in 1912 on a sky city (named Columbia) , Elizabeth is to take over the city once its current leader (or "Prophet"), Father Comstock, dies. Elizabeth can make and exploit "tears", rips to other universes, allowing her to move between them and/or bring in items from them. To prevent her from leaving Columbia, a remote "Siphon" is made which limits her powers, and she is locked in a tower under study and guarded by a giant mechanical bird known as Songbird.
generated_background_story: Vera Castellan was trained as a deep-range reconnaissance operative for a military unit that no longer officially exists. After her squad was ambushed during what should have been a routine extraction, she spent three years surviving alone in hostile territory with limited supplies and no radio contact. The experience hardened her into a pragmatic survivor who treats every environment as a potential battlefield and every weapon as a tool for a specific job. She eventually found her way to the frontier region where the game takes place, drawn by rumors that the corporation responsible for her unit's destruction has established operations in the area. Vera approaches each new environment with cold calculation, mentally cataloguing sight lines for sniping positions and choke points for close-quarters engagements. She struggles to connect with others after years of isolation, preferring to work alone even when allies would make survival easier. The open terrain and cramped industrial ruins of this new region offer both opportunities and dangers that test her full range of combat skills.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Quick Swap: Reduces weapon switching time by 40% for rapid adaptation to changing combat situations.
  - Steady Aim: Decreases weapon sway when aiming down sights, improving accuracy at medium range.
  - Combat Roll: Allows a quick evasive roll in any direction to dodge incoming fire.
- Tier 2:
  - Precision Optics: Highlights enemy weak points through environmental obstacles for a brief duration.
  - Close Quarters Mastery: Increases melee damage and grants faster execution animations in tight spaces.
  - Extended Mags: Increases ammunition capacity for all carried weapons by 25%.
- Tier 3:
  - Ghost Protocol: Reduces footstep noise and suppresses muzzle flash, enabling stealthier approaches.
  - Chain Reaction: Defeating an enemy causes nearby hostiles within range to become briefly disoriented.
  - Hunter's Resolve: Regenerates a portion of health after achieving multiple consecutive kills without taking damage.

original_background_story:
Alan Wake was born and raised in New York City. He developed a passion for writing at a young age, eventually leading to a successful novelist career. His most famous works are thriller novels, which have earned him a large and dedicated following. Despite his success, Alan struggles with writer's block and personal issues, including a strained relationship with his wife, Alice.

In an attempt to overcome his writer's block, Alan and Alice take a vacation to the idyllic town of Bright Falls. However, their peaceful retreat quickly turns into a nightmare when Alice disappears under mysterious circumstances. Alan discovers pages of a manuscript he doesn't remember writing, detailing events that start to come true in real life.

As he searches for Alice, Alan encounters dark supernatural forces known as the "Taken," shadowy entities controlled by a malevolent presence called the "Darkness." He learns that the town and its surroundings are influenced by this dark power, which has trapped him in a nightmarish reality where his own writing becomes a weapon and a tool for survival.

A crucial figure in Alan's journey is Thomas Zane, a poet who lived in Bright Falls decades earlier and had a similar encounter with the Darkness. Zane’s writings and his struggle against the Darkness provide Alan with clues and guidance on how to fight supernatural forces.

In the end, Alan realizes that to save Alice and stop the Darkness, he must make a significant sacrifice. He writes a conclusion to the horror story he is trapped in, allowing Alice to escape while he remains in the dark, nightmarish world beneath Cauldron Lake. This act of self-sacrifice shows his character's deep love for his wife and his determination to end the cycle of terror.
generated_background_story: Elias Vorne built his career crafting pulse-pounding thrillers that blurred the line between psychological unraveling and supernatural dread, earning him a reputation as a master of late-night page-turners. But three years of silence followed his last novel, a creative drought that strained his marriage and eroded his confidence. Hoping to reignite his spark, Elias agreed to retreat with his wife Clara to the remote mist-shrouded town of Hollowbrook, where the endless gray skies and towering pines promised solitude. Hours after their arrival, Clara vanished during a walk to the general store, leaving behind only her scarf tangled in a fence. Desperate and disbelieving, Elias began finding typewritten manuscript pages scattered through the forest—pages written in his own voice, describing events moments before they occurred. The words bled into reality, and shadow-slicked figures emerged from every dark corner, whispering fragments of stories he never finished writing. Armed with only a flashlight and the fragments of a narrative he cannot remember creating, Elias must uncover who—or what—is authoring his nightmare before the final chapter closes on him forever.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Focused Beam: Concentrates your flashlight into a narrow high-intensity beam that stuns shadow enemies and reveals hidden manuscript pages.
  - Flare Toss: Throws a handheld flare that creates a temporary circle of safety, repelling darkness and illuminating nearby collectibles.
  - Author's Instinct: Highlights interactive objects and environmental clues within a short radius through a faint golden shimmer.
- Tier 2:
  - Battery Efficiency: Reduces flashlight drain by forty percent, allowing extended use of light-based attacks and exploration tools.
  - Evasive Roll: Performs a quick combat roll in any direction, granting brief invulnerability frames and creating distance from enemies.
  - Margin Notes: Automatically annotates discovered manuscript pages with clues, revealing weak points of specific enemy types.
- Tier 3:
  - Revision: Rewinds the last eight seconds of gameplay, restoring health and ammo while resetting enemy positions and environmental states.
  - Blinding Cascade: Unleashes stored battery power in a 360-degree burst of light, stunning all nearby enemies and dissolving weaker shadows.
  - Narrative Arc: Predicts incoming ambushes and environmental hazards five seconds in advance, displaying ghostly silhouettes of threats through walls.

original_background_story:
Born in april 9 1967 
lives in a Penthouse in Las Vegas, Nevada. 
Saved the world from alien invasion multiple time, work with the EDF (Earth Defence Force)
generated_background_story: Cade Vortex was a top operative for the Temporal Defense Coalition until a sabotaged mission left him stranded in a shattered timeline where humanity faces extinction across multiple eras. The explosion that displaced him also embedded chronal energy into his DNA, granting him enhanced reflexes and the ability to sense rifts between time periods. Driven by revenge against the traitor who set him up, Cade now works as a mercenary, selling his unique talents to the highest bidder while secretly hunting for clues to his betrayer's identity. His internal conflict stems from his growing attachment to the people he meets across timelines—knowing he can never stay, he struggles between emotional detachment and his innate desire to protect the innocent. The stakes rise when he discovers that the same conspiracy that destroyed his timeline is systematically collapsing others, and only someone who exists outside normal time can stop it. Cade's attitude and combat skills make him the perfect weapon for this temporal war, but his heart may be his ultimate weakness.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Rift Dash: Instantly teleport a short distance forward, passing through solid objects and enemies.
  - Overcharge: Temporarily double weapon fire rate for 5 seconds with reduced accuracy.
  - Salvage: Recover ammunition from defeated enemies automatically.
- Tier 2:
  - Time Echo: Spawn a decoy that mimics your last action to confuse and distract enemies.
  - Hardlight Shield: Project a frontal energy barrier that absorbs incoming damage.

original_background_story:
Zinyak is an extraterrestrial warlord and the bad guy in Saint's Row IV. He's the leader of the alien race known as the Zin. Sounding very posh, he speaks with an upper class English accent and is a big fan of literature. It was actually the love for books that brought him to Earth. Zinyak often makes references from famous work, such as Macbeth and Edgar Allan Poe. 

During the events of the game, he invades Earth and comes through the ceiling of the White House where he abducts Kinzie Kensington and Shaundi before disappearing. He also defeats the Boss, who is now the President of the United States, and puts him/her into a virtual simulation based on their worst fears. 

Zinyak live streams everything to his planet where all the Zin can watch how he slowly but surely kills the humans and erases mankind. His goal is to take over, not really thinking that anyone on Earth can be strong enough to stop him.
generated_background_story:
Jace Verran grew up as a street-smart orphan in the industrial district of Neos Veridia, scraping by through underground fighting rings and small-time courier jobs. His reputation for getting impossible jobs done caught the attention of the Rising Dawn collective, a grassroots organization that fought against corrupt corporate control. Over a decade, Jace rose from a low-level runner to the face of the movement, uniting fractured neighborhoods under a banner of resistance and reform. When the movement transitioned from revolutionary to political, public support carried him into the presidential seat of the Veridian Territories. His presidency marked a rare era of transparency and social progress—until the Zyxari Imperium descended from the skies without warning.

The alien invaders didn't just conquer; they digitized, trapping humanity's consciousness inside a massive simulation called the Shroud. Jace awoke inside this virtual prison to find his memories fragmented and his companions missing, scattered across corrupted sectors. The simulation is controlled by the Zyxari overlords, who study human behavior like specimens in a digital petri dish. Guided by a rogue program embedded in his psyche, Jace must navigate glitching cityscapes, exploit the simulation's vulnerabilities, and free his allies before their digital forms degrade. Every step closer to freedom reveals uncomfortable truths about what remains of the real world.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Data Rip: Jace tears a fragment of code from enemies, dealing minor damage and briefly revealing their position.
  - Glitch Dash: A short-range burst of speed that phases Jace through solid objects for half a second.
  - Memory Cache: Accesses hidden data nodes to restore a small amount of health and digital integrity.
- Tier 2:

  - Overclock Reflexes: Temporarily slows perceived time, allowing Jace to dodge incoming attacks with precision.

original_background_story: Peach Wilkins is a resident of Neptune's Bounty and seems to be one of the leaders of Frank Fontaine's smuggler group.
generated_background_story: Silas Drift was once a maintenance engineer responsible for keeping the underwater city's life-support systems functioning, until he discovered the ruling council was deliberately sabotaging oxygen supplies to cull the lower districts. Exposed to experimental biological mutagens during the facility's collapse, Silas developed unstable abilities that allow him to interface directly with ancient technology. Now he scours the flooded ruins for artifacts capable of reversing the city's systematic decay. His body slowly deteriorates from the mutagens coursing through his veins, giving him limited time to find a cure. The city's factions each offer him contradictory salvation, and he must decide whose technology is worth trusting. Every new modification pushes him further from humanity, yet he clings desperately to the few memories of the surface world that still haunt him. The deeper he descends, the more the whispers of the old machines promise him power—and threaten to erase what remains of his soul.
generated_skill_tree:

original_background_story: Daxter is a central character in the "Jak and Daxter" video game series. Originally a human, Daxter is transformed into an ottsel, a fictional hybrid of an otter and a weasel, after falling into a pool of Dark Eco. Known for his witty and outspoken personality, Daxter serves as Jak's loyal sidekick and best friend. Despite his small stature, he is resourceful, brave, and provides comic relief throughout their adventures. Daxter's unique transformation and humorous nature make him a memorable and indispensable part of the duo.
generated_background_story: Zane was an ordinary teenager living in a secluded seaside hamlet until an expedition to a forbidden silo went wrong. His best friend, a reckless tinkerer, was exposed to a volatile energy source and transformed into a small, orange otter-weasel hybrid. Zane swears to find a way to reverse the transformation, carrying his friend on his shoulder as they traverse the continent. They must navigate ancient ruins, collect scattered power cores, and appease the local sages to unlock the path to the cure. Zane’s internal conflict lies in his stoic determination; while his friend babbles endlessly, Zane internalizes the guilt of the accident. The stakes grow as the dark energy begins to corrupt the land, forcing them to move quickly. Zane relies on his agility and fighting instincts to protect his transformed companion.
generated_skill_tree:

  - Double Jump: Allows Zane to leap again in mid-air to reach higher ledges.
  - Spin Kick: A rapid rotational attack that knocks back nearby enemies.
  - Swim: Enables traversal through deep water bodies without taking damage.
- Tier 2:
  - Dash Punch: A quick forward lunge that breaks weak barriers and damages foes.
  - Uppercut: Launches enemies into the air, allowing for follow-up combos.

original_background_story:
Tommy, an Italian-American, is portrayed with a tall, swarthy, handsome appearance, combed dark brown, almost black hair, and a constant five o'clock shadow. He first appears wearing a light blue-green Hawaiian shirt with dark blue palm trees printed on it, a gold pearl necklace around his neck, a gold watch around his left wrist, and a pair of blue jeans and white sneakers. This outfit becomes known as Tommy’s "street" clothes. As the game progresses, Tommy is offered more wardrobe options: he obtains a blue-purple pastel suit with the sleeves rolled up over a black dress shirt and black loafers, as well as the option of a dark pinstripe suit labeled as "Mr. Vercetti" (which bears similarities to Tony Montana's uniform). A light green costume is available to perform a bank robbery, complete with a hockey mask, and is even able to wear the uniform for an allied gang, among many others.

Tommy Vercetti, in several ways, exhibits characteristics of fictional drug lord Tony Montana from the 1983 film Scarface. This coincides with the heavy themes and appearance of the movie that has been implemented into Vice City. Among these characteristics, his exile from his old home (Liberty City), his rise to power (acquiring property and wealth in the city, and a mansion which also sports an interior similar to that of Montana’s mansion). Tommy is also a hired assassin, has killed his own collaborators (Lance Vance), taken over his temporary boss’s business (Ricardo Diaz) and rebelled against his former leader (Sonny Forelli), as Tony Montana had. The only notable difference is that the final gunfight in Montana's mansion sees Montana eventually killed, whereas the final gunfight in Tommy's mansion sees Tommy managing to single-handedly take down his captors and survive.
generated_background_story: Rico Valez arrived in the sun-soaked metropolis of Bay City with nothing but a duffel bag and a shattered reputation. After a smuggling operation in the Caribbean went sideways, he found himself deeply indebted to a powerful crime syndicate known as The Trust. Instead of fleeing, Rico decided to carve out his own slice of paradise amidst the neon glow and art deco architecture. He navigates a treacherous landscape of rival gangs and corrupt officials, using stolen vehicles and black-market weaponry to stay alive. His ultimate goal is to dismantle The Trust's leadership and claim the city's underworld for himself. The sun-drenched streets and synth-wave nights provide the perfect backdrop for his violent ascent to power. Rico struggles with the moral weight of his actions, knowing that every step forward costs innocent lives. As he climbs the ladder, he realizes that in Bay City, the only way to survive is to become the very thing the city worships: power.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Wheelman: Allows the character to enter vehicles significantly faster than average.
  - Street Smarts: Reveals hidden collectibles and secret paths on the mini-map.
  - Hot Start: Spawns the character with a basic sidearm and full health after a respawn.
- Tier 2:
  - Bulletproof Vest: Reduces incoming firearm damage by 25% while wearing armor.
  - Lead Foot: Increases the top speed of all ground vehicles by 15%.
  - Quick Draw: Doubles the firing rate of pistols and revolvers.
- Tier 3:
  - Kingpin: Unlocks the ability to recruit AI allies to assist in combat missions.
  - Wrecking Ball: Causes vehicles to explode on impact with enemy targets.
  - Time Out: Slows down time for precise aiming during intense firefights.

original_background_story: Rintarou Okabe (岡部 倫太郁) is a self-proclaimed mad scientist and the main protagonist of Steins;Gate. He is a student of Tokyo Electronics University and the founder of Future Gadget Laboratory, a small research laboratory devoted into creation of future gadgets for the betterment of mankind. He also likes to pose as an agent working against a certain "Organization" which he thinks is always after him, as he believes this kind of image gives him a cool and mysterious aura. He is one of the (accidental) inventors of the Future Gadget #8, Phone Microwave, which has the ability to send data back in time.
generated_background_story: Sora Kitani is a brilliant but reclusive engineering student attending a prestigious technical institute in the heart of a bustling metropolis. Obsessed with the concept of retrocausality, Sora spends days modifying obsolete electronics to detect anomalies in the local time-space continuum. Her research leads her to discover a hidden frequency that allows her phone to receive text messages from the near future, dragging her into a shadowy conflict involving a clandestine research organization. Motivated by the accidental death of her older brother years prior, she seeks to rewrite the past and uncover the truth behind the city's strange temporal disturbances. Internally, Sora struggles with the ethical ramifications of altering timelines, fearing that saving her brother might erase the existence of her closest friends. She operates from a cramped apartment filled with CRT monitors and soldering equipment, serving as the reluctant operator for a group of friends investigating the phenomenon. The stakes rise when she realizes her own existence is becoming unstable, phasing in and out of reality with every major change she initiates. Sora must navigate complex branching scenarios, using her intellect to outmaneuver agents of the organization while managing the emotional toll of her choices.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Temporal Ping: Reveals hidden objects or clues in the immediate environment by highlighting them for a short duration.
  - Logic Divert: Allows Sora to solve environmental puzzles instantly by analyzing circuit patterns.
  - Sprint Burst: Increases movement speed temporarily to evade pursuit or reach timed objectives.
- Tier 2:
  - Future Glance: Peeks at the outcome of a dialogue choice, showing if it leads to a negative or positive immediate result.
  - Memory Overwrite: Changes a minor NPC's perception of an event, opening new dialogue paths.
  - Signal Jam: Disables electronic surveillance cameras in a small radius for a limited time.
- Tier 3:
  - Causality Loop: Rewinds time by thirty seconds in a specific room to retry a failed action or puzzle.
  - World Line Shift: Unlocks a hidden dialogue option that drastically alters the story branch.
  - Quantum Stabilize: Prevents a "Game Over" scenario once per playthrough by stabilizing a fatal injury.

original_background_story:
Ursula is a 32 year old female Newman and a supporting character in Phantasy Star II & Phantasy Star Portable 2. Newmans are a race evolved from humans in the universe. They were created for research purposes, but eventually became a race of their own. She is a brilliant and sharp woman with many talents. She spent many years in the Alliance Military Force, where she met Kraz Muehler. They fell in love and decided to marry each other.

After being an instructor of AMF, she became a leader of Little Wing and her beauty mixed with her talents sent out a message of respect and dignity. Ursula however, often hides her feelings, leaving her with many regrets about her past. She's often sad about a lot of choices and thinks about them a lot. 

Ursula is also a fashion designer.
generated_background_story: Dravos Ironhand was once the champion of the Grand Colosseum, undefeated for seven seasons until a corrupt noble paid his own stablemaster to ensure a losing match. Left for dead in the aftermath and sold into indentured servitude, Dravos spent years in the fighting pits of the outer provinces, honing his craft and biding his time. He eventually earned his freedom through a series of brutal exhibition matches, emerging with a reputation as a fighter who never surrenders and never breaks. Now returned to the capital city, Dravos seeks to build his own gladiator school from nothing, recruiting society's castoffs and forging them into champions. He is driven by a desire for vengeance against the noble who wronged him, but struggles with the morality of sending others into the arena to achieve his goals. The memory of every fighter he has lost haunts him, and he carries a small stone token from his first fallen recruit as a reminder of the cost of ambition. Dravos believes that glory earned through honor is the only glory worth having, even in a world that rewards treachery.
generated_skill_tree:

  - Iron Guard: Reduces incoming damage by 15% for one turn when defending.
  - Rallying Shout: Grants a small attack bonus to all allies for the next round.
  - Shoulder Charge: Deals moderate damage and has a chance to stagger the enemy.
- Tier 2:

  - Veteran's Insight: Reveals enemy weakness and increases critical hit chance by 10%.

  - Colosseum Legend: Inspires all allies, granting them immunity to fear effects and a major defense boost for three turns.
  - Final Verdict: Delivers a powerful finishing strike that deals massive damage to enemies below 25% health.

original_background_story:
Ryu Hayabusa is the protagonist in the Ninja Gaiden series developed by Team Ninja and published by Tecmo. He also appears in almost every mainline games of the Dead or Alive series. 
 
As a ninja, Ryu ruthlessly kills his enemies without mercy, executing them in a very cold, technical and calculative fashion. His rigorous upbringing in tandem with the many hardships and tragedies he has suffered, has forced Ryu to harden his emotions and disciplined his skills to perfection. He is undaunted by the lives he must take in order to protect the balance within the world, knowing that the balance of the world is more important. 
 
Ryu knows life following the ninja path can be both cruel and short, especially for one of the Dragon Lineage. He has already felt first hand the brutality of this life; watching Kureha, his best friend murdered right in front of his own eyes, his clan massacred and he himself killed as he tries to save his village. Ryu endures these hardships with his unstoppable will, an indomitable spirit shown in his exploits as he overcomes every challenge and obstacle, surviving even the trauma of death to return and exact his vengeance. 
 
Though Ryu cares little for his enemies' well being, he still respects them on a warrior basis, even having mutual respect with some adversaries such as Genshin. Outside of battle, Ryu is modest and refined, he is popular among the local residents in his village. Not only as a strong role model for ninja in training, but he is heralded for being kind and gentle, as he cares for the safety of every resident in his home. This is due to the fact that each Hayabusa clansman from Jonin to cadet is cherished as family.
generated_background_story: Soren Vale was raised in a coastal village known for its ancient fighting traditions, where practitioners learned to adapt their techniques across varied terrains including frozen lakes and flooded arenas. His father, a legendary competitor, vanished during a mysterious tournament that took place across a series of interconnected island stages. Soren spent years honing his skills in the circular combat system, mastering the interplay between strikes, throws, and counter-holds that defines his family's martial art. Now he enters the tournament circuit seeking answers, driven by rumors that his father still competes in underground matches held in secret multi-tiered locations. His internal conflict centers on whether to embrace the brutal efficiency needed to win or maintain the honor his father taught him. The stakes rise when he discovers the tournament organizers are collecting fighters with exceptional counter abilities for an unknown purpose. Soren must decide how far he will go to uncover the truth.

  - Wall Awareness: Grants brief damage reduction when cornered against stage walls.
  - Recovery Stance: Reduces vulnerability frames after missed throw attempts.

  - Arena Mastery: Falling between stage tiers deals less damage and grants brief speed boost upon landing.
  - Final Determination: Once per round, surviving a potential knockout blow leaves Soren at minimal health with a temporary attack boost.

original_background_story: Kasumi (means "haze" or "mist") Nakano (means "in the field") is a 19 year old human female that appears in the Fallout 4's add-on Far Harbor. You'll find her on the Island in 2287. She is curious, naive and gets easily bored. Kasumi and her grandfather had a large mechanical knowledge and started to restore pre-War appliances around her house together. After restoring a radio, she contacts DiMa, where she is led to believe that she is actually a synth. Odd dreams and her abilities also reinforces this belief. Later, Kasumi flees Acadia and it's her future is based on the player's choice.
generated_background_story: Born in the suffocating depths of Sector 7, Vesper has never known a life outside the heavy industrial chains of the penal colony. They were conscripted into the Organization's retrieval teams at the age of sixteen, forced to scavenge technology from rival factions in exchange for reduced time on their life sentence. Vesper fights not out of loyalty, but out of a desperate need to earn a "Clean-Air Passport" and escape the toxic smog that claimed their parents. During a high-stakes rescue mission, Vesper discovered a civilian child hiding data that could dismantle the Organization's control over the remaining resources. Now, they face an internal conflict between following orders to secure the data for their masters or protecting the innocent to spite the system. Every mission inches them closer to freedom or a premature grave in the wasteland dust.

original_background_story:
Lara Croft is a fictional character and the protagonist of the Square Enix (previously Eidos Interactive) video game franchise Tomb Raider. She is presented as a beautiful, intelligent, and athletic English archaeologist-adventurer who ventures into ancient, hazardous tombs and ruins around the world. Created by a team at UK developer Core Design that included Toby Gard, the character first appeared in the 1996 video game Tomb Raider. She has also appeared in video game sequels, printed adaptations, a series of animated short films, feature films (portrayed by Angelina Jolie), and merchandise related to the series. Official promotion of the character includes a brand of apparel and accessories, action figures, and model portrayals. Croft has also been licensed for third-party promotion, including television and print advertisements, music-related appearances, and as a spokesmodel.

Core Design handled initial development of the character and the series. Inspired by Neneh Cherry and comic book character Tank Girl, Gard designed Lara Croft to counter stereotypical female characters. The company modified the character for subsequent titles, which included graphical improvements and gameplay additions. American developer Crystal Dynamics took over the series after the 2003 sequel Tomb Raider: The Angel of Darkness was received poorly. The new developer rebooted the character along with the video game series. The company altered her physical proportions, and gave her additional ways of interacting with game environments. Croft has been voiced by six actresses in the video game series: Shelley Blond (1996), Judith Gibbins (1997–98), Jonell Elliott (1999–2003), Keeley Hawes (2006–14), Camilla Luddington (2013–present) and Abigail Stahlschmidt (2015).

Critics consider Lara Croft a significant game character in popular culture. She holds six Guinness World Records, has a strong fan following, and is among the first video game characters to be successfully adapted to film.[3] Lara Croft is also considered a sex symbol, one of the earliest in the industry to achieve widespread attention. The character's influence in the industry has been a point of contention among critics; viewpoints range from a positive agent of change in video games to a negative role model for young girls.
generated_background_story: Elara Vance was a promising anthropology student who joined an expedition to the forgotten isle of Aethelgard, hoping to prove herself to her estranged father. When her crew's vessel capsized during a unnatural storm, she washed ashore alone, separated from her friends and stripped of modern equipment. Now, armed with nothing but a salvaged machete and her father's old journal, she must navigate the crumbling temples of a civilization that supposedly never existed. The island tests more than her physical endurance; it preys on her deepest fears, manifesting hallucinations that blur the line between reality and nightmare. As she uncovers the violent history of the Guardians who once ruled here, Elara realizes she isn't just fighting for escape, but to prevent an ancient darkness from spreading to the outside world. Every scar she earns transforms her from a naive academic into a hardened survivor, forcing her to decide how much of her humanity she is willing to sacrifice to see the sunrise again.

original_background_story:
Nathan Drake, born Nathan Morgan, had a tumultuous childhood. Orphaned at a young age, he and his older brother Sam were raised in a Catholic orphanage. The brothers shared a passion for history and treasure hunting, inspired by stories from their mother, a historian. Believing they are descended from the famous English explorer Sir Francis Drake, Nathan adopts the Drake surname.

Nathan's adult life is marked by a series of high-stakes adventures around the world, searching for legendary artefacts and lost cities. His escapades are often accompanied by his mentor and friend Victor "Sully" Sullivan, journalist Elena Fisher, and his brother Sam. Nathan's adventures take him from the jungles of South America to the deserts of the Arabian Peninsula and the Himalayas' peaks.
generated_background_story: Elias Voss grew up listening to his grandmother's tales of their ancestor, a navigator who vanished seeking a legendary artifact said to grant foresight. For years, Elias dismissed these as folklore while building a career as a salvage diver, recovering lost cargo from shipwrecks along the Atlantic coast. When his grandmother passed, he inherited her belongings and discovered a sealed envelope containing a 400-year-old navigator's log—the last recorded entries of his missing ancestor. The log's final coordinates pointed to an uncharted island chain rumored to be cursed. Driven by family duty and professional curiosity, Elias assembled a small team and embarked on the expedition, only for a violent storm to wreck their vessel on approach. Now stranded with limited supplies, he must protect his companions from a ruthless mercenary faction that arrived before them. Elias fights not just for survival, but to uncover whether his ancestor found the artifact or fell to the island's secrets.
